valueStreams.name: Create the optimal recipe
valueStreams.description: Build a formulation that satisfies nutrient and business constraints at minimal cost.

stages.name: Select candidate ingredients
stages.description: Choose available and approved raw materials based on nutrition, price, supplier status and inventory.

stages.name: Apply nutritional and operational constraints
stages.description: Define limits, ratios, inclusion rules, customer specs, processing constraints and plant restrictions.

stages.name: Run optimization
stages.description: Execute least-cost or multi-objective calculation to generate viable formulas.

stages.name: Evaluate alternatives
stages.description: Compare options by cost, risk, availability, quality history and production practicality.

stages.name: Validate plant practicality
stages.description: Confirm batching feasibility, pelletability, physical properties and process impact.

valueStreams.name: Execute the plan reliably
valueStreams.description: Deliver production according to schedule while managing real-world constraints.

stages.name: Receive production schedule
stages.description: Obtain prioritized orders, sequences and quantities from planning.

stages.name: Confirm materials and capacity
stages.description: Verify raw material availability, staffing and equipment readiness.

stages.name: Dispatch orders to lines
stages.description: Release instructions and recipes to operators or automation.

stages.name: Manage emerging issues
stages.description: Handle shortages, breakdowns or quality holds and re-plan if necessary.

stages.name: Record actuals
stages.description: Capture real consumption, times, yields and deviations.

valueStreams.name: Release safe and compliant product
valueStreams.description: Ensure products meet specifications and legal requirements before shipment.

stages.name: Define sampling requirements
stages.description: Set which tests are required based on risk, material and regulations.

stages.name: Receive and validate lab results
stages.description: Collect results automatically or manually and ensure completeness.

stages.name: Compare against limits
stages.description: Evaluate conformity versus specifications and tolerances.

stages.name: Block or release product
stages.description: Decide disposition and communicate to operations and logistics.

stages.name: Generate required documentation
stages.description: Produce COAs, compliance statements and audit evidence.

valueStreams.name: Keep the landscape stable and integrated
valueStreams.description: Ensure systems are reliable, secure and connected.

stages.name: Maintain interfaces
stages.description: Ensure data flows between ERP, formulation, MES, labs and partners.

stages.name: Monitor incidents and performance
stages.description: Track uptime, latency, failures and respond quickly.

stages.name: Manage upgrades and releases
stages.description: Plan deployments while minimizing disruption.

stages.name: Enforce security and governance
stages.description: Apply roles, auditability, data protection and compliance.

stages.name: Reduce shadow IT
stages.description: Provide trusted capabilities so users avoid spreadsheets or side tools.

operatingModelNotes: The Nutrition enterprise architecture domain covers the business, application, data, and technology structures needed to design, manufacture, govern, and improve nutrition solutions under strict quality and compliance constraints.
Business capabilities should be mapped to accountable owners and supported by governed application services.
Cross-domain dependencies should be handled through explicit value streams and service contracts.
